message.content:
In /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic, explore the full scope of what needs accuracy testing:

1. Read libm/math/ directory listing to see ALL generic math function source files (both s_*.c and sf_*.c).
2. Read libm/common/ directory listing for classification/utility functions.
3. Check if there are any existing test vector files or reference data files in the test/ directory.
4. Look at test/test-efcvt.c and test/test-strtod.c for examples of tests that check specific values.
5. Check if there's an MPFR or other high-precision reference library available or used anywhere.
6. Look at how the test infrastructure handles expected failures and tolerances.
7. Check the picolibc test output format - how do tests report pass/fail?

I need to understand what accuracy testing infrastructure exists and what we'd need to build.

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough exploration of the accuracy testing infrastructure in picolibc. Let me start with multiple parallel searches to gather information about the math functions and testing setup.
Now let me get the full count and check the common directory:

content.text: Now let me explore the test directory for existing test infrastructure:
Good! Now let me read the key test files to understand the testing patterns:

content.content:

    35→
    36→#define _GNU_SOURCE
    37→#include <stdlib.h>
    38→#include <stdio.h>
    39→#include <string.h>
    40→
    41→struct test {
    42→    double value;
    43→    int    ndigit;
    44→    int    decpt;
    45→    int    sign;
    46→    char  *expect;
    47→};
    48→
    49→const struct test ecvt_tests[] = {
    50→    {
    51→     1.0e0, 4,
    52→     1, 0,
    53→     "1000", },
    54→    {
    55→     -1.0e0,
    56→     4, 1,
    57→     1, "1000",
    58→     },
    59→    { 1.0e7, 7, 8, 0, "1000000" },
    60→    { 1.0e-12, 4, -11, 0, "1000" },
    61→};
    62→
    63→#if ((__GNUC__ == 4 && __GNUC_MINOR__ >= 2) || __GNUC__ > 4)
    64→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wpragmas"
    65→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunknown-warning-option"
    66→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunused-value"
    67→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Woverflow"
    68→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wliteral-range"
    69→#endif
    70→
    71→#define N_ECVT_TESTS (sizeof(ecvt_tests) / sizeof(ecvt_tests[0]))
    72→
    73→const struct test fcvt_tests[] = {
    74→    {
    75→     0.0058882729652625027, 7,
    76→     -2,
    77→     0, "58883",
    78→     },
    79→    {
    80→     1.0e0, 4,
    81→     1, 0,
    82→     "10000", },
    83→    {
    84→     -1.0e0,
    85→     4, 1,
    86→     1, "10000",
    87→     },
    88→    { 1.0e7, 7, 8, 0, "100000000000000" },
    89→    { 1.0e-12, 4, -4, 0, "" },
    90→    { 1.23456e0, -4, 1, 0, "1" },
    91→    { 1.23456e1, -4, 2, 0, "10" },
    92→    { 1.23456e2, -4, 3, 0, "100" },
    93→    { 1.23456e3, -4, 4, 0, "1000" },
    94→    { 1.23456e4, -4, 5, 0, "10000" },
    95→    { 1.23456e5, -4, 6, 0, "120000" },
    96→    { 1.23456e6, -4, 7, 0, "1230000" },
    97→    { 1.23456e-10, 4, -4, 0, "" },
    98→    { 1.23456e-9, 4, -4, 0, "" },
    99→    { 1.23456e-8, 4, -4, 0, "" },
   100→    { 1.23456e-7, 4, -4, 0, "" },
   101→    { 1.23456e-6, 4, -4, 0, "" },
   102→    { 1.23456e-5, 4, -4, 0, "" },
   103→    { 1.23456e-4, 4, -3, 0, "1" },
   104→    { 1.23456e-3, 4, -2, 0, "12" },
   105→    { 1.23456e-2, 4, -1, 0, "123" },
   106→    { 1.23456e-1, 4, 0, 0, "1235" },
   107→    { 1.23456e0, 4, 1, 0, "12346" },
   108→    { 1.23456e1, 4, 2, 0, "123456" },
   109→    { 1.23456e2, 4, 3, 0, "1234560" },
   110→    { 1.23456e3, 4, 4, 0, "12345600" },
   111→    { 1.23456e4, 4, 5, 0, "123456000" },
   112→    { 1.23456e5, 4, 6, 0, "1234560000" },
   113→    { 1.23456e6, 4, 7, 0, "12345600000" },
   114→    { 1.23456e7, 4, 8, 0, "123456000000" },
   115→    { 1.23456e8, 4, 9, 0, "1234560000000" },
   116→    { 1.23456e9, 4, 10, 0, "12345600000000" },
   117→};
   118→
   119→#define N_FCVT_TESTS (sizeof(fcvt_tests) / sizeof(fcvt_tests[0]))
   120→
   121→const struct test fcvt_extra_tests[] = {
   122→#ifdef __PICOLIBC__
   123→#if __SIZEOF_DOUBLE__ == 4
   124→    { 0x1.fffffep127, 9, 39, 0, "340282350000000000000000000000000000000000000000" },
   125→#else
   126→    { 0x1.fffffffffffffp1023, 17, 309, 0,
   127→      "17976931348623157"
   128→      "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
   129→      "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
   130→      "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
   131→      "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
   132→      "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
   133→      "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
   134→      "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000"
   135→      "000000000000"
   136→      "00000000000000000" },
   137→    { 0x1.fffffep127, 9, 39, 0, "340282346638528860000000000000000000000000000000" },
   138→#endif
   139→#else
   140→    { 0x1.fffffffffffffp1023, 17, 309, 0,
   141→      "17976931348623157"
   142→      "0814527423731704356798070567525844996598"
   143→      "9174768031572607800285387605895586327668"
   144→      "7817154045895351438246423432132688946418"
   145→      "2768467546703537516986049910576551282076"
   146→      "2454900903893289440758685084551339423045"
   147→      "8323690322294816580855933212334827479782"
   148→      "6204144723168738177180919299881250404026"
   149→      "184124858368"
   150→      "00000000000000000" },
   151→    { 0x1.fffffep127, 9, 39, 0, "340282346638528859811704183484516925440000000000" },
   152→#endif
   153→};
   154→
   155→#define N_FCVT_EXTRA_TESTS (sizeof(fcvt_extra_tests) / sizeof(fcvt_extra_tests[0]))
   156→
   157→const struct test fcvtf_tests[] = {
   158→#ifdef __PICOLIBC__
   159→    { 0x1.fffffep127, 9, 39, 0, "340282350000000000000000000000000000000000000000" },
   160→#else
   161→    /* glibc uses the double path for this operation */
   162→    { 0x1.fffffep127, 9, 39, 0, "340282346638528859811704183484516925440000000000" },
   163→#endif
   164→};
   165→
   166→#define N_FCVTF_TESTS (sizeof(fcvtf_tests) / sizeof(fcvtf_tests[0]))
   167→
   168→#if defined(__PICOLIBC__) && !defined(__IO_FLOAT_EXACT)
   169→/* non-exact conversions are not precise over about 6 digits */
   170→#define SKIP_LONG_FLOAT 1
   171→#else
   172→#define SKIP_LONG_FLOAT 0
   173→#endif
   174→
   175→#if (!defined(__IO_FLOAT_EXACT)) && __SIZEOF_DOUBLE__ < 8
   176→#undef SKIP_LONG_FLOAT
   177→#define SKIP_LONG_FLOAT    1
   178→#define SKIP_LONGISH_FLOAT 1
   179→#endif
   180→
   181→#ifndef SKIP_LONGISH_FLOAT
   182→#define SKIP_LONGISH_FLOAT 0
   183→#endif
   184→
   185→#define many_tests(tests, func, n, skip_long)                                                    \
   186→    do {                                                                                         \
   187→        for (i = 0; i < n; i++) {                                                                \
   188→            int decpt;                                                                           \
   189→            int sign;                                                                            \
   190→            int ret = func(tests[i].value, tests[i].ndigit, &decpt, &sign, buf, sizeof(buf));    \
   191→                                                                                                 \
   192→            if (ret < 0) {                                                                       \
   193→                printf("%d: failed\n", ret);                                                     \
   194→                continue;                                                                        \
   195→            }                                                                                    \
   196→            if (skip_long && strlen(buf) > 6) {                                                  \
   197→                printf("skipping test as result is long (%s)\n", buf);                           \
   198→                continue;                                                                        \
   199→            }                                                                                    \
   200→            if (strcmp(buf, tests[i].expect) != 0 || decpt != tests[i].decpt                     \
   201→                || sign != tests[i].sign) {                                                      \
   202→                printf(#func ":%d got '%s' dec %d sign %d expect '%s' dec %d sign %d\n", i, buf, \
   203→                       decpt, sign, tests[i].expect, tests[i].decpt, tests[i].sign);             \
   204→                error = 1;                                                                       \
   205→            }                                                                                    \
   206→        }                                                                                        \
   207→    } while (0)
   208→
   209→int
   210→main(void)
   211→{
   212→    int         error = 0;
   213→    unsigned    i;
   214→    static char buf[2048];
   215→
   216→    many_tests(fcvt_tests, fcvt_r, N_FCVT_TESTS, SKIP_LONGISH_FLOAT);
   217→    many_tests(fcvt_extra_tests, fcvt_r, N_FCVT_EXTRA_TESTS, SKIP_LONG_FLOAT);
   218→    many_tests(ecvt_tests, ecvt_r, N_ECVT_TESTS, SKIP_LONGISH_FLOAT);
   219→#ifdef __PICOLIBC__
   220→    many_tests(fcvt_tests, fcvtf_r, N_FCVT_TESTS, SKIP_LONG_FLOAT);
   221→    many_tests(fcvtf_tests, fcvtf_r, N_FCVTF_TESTS, SKIP_LONG_FLOAT);
   222→    many_tests(ecvt_tests, ecvtf_r, N_ECVT_TESTS, SKIP_LONGISH_FLOAT);
   223→#endif
   224→    return error;
   225→}
   226→

content.text: Good! Now let me look at the test-real-one.h framework:
Now let me check the test-math.h header and look for sample vector data:

content.content:
1→/*
     2→ * SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause
     3→ *
     4→ * Copyright © 2025 Keith Packard
     5→ *
     6→ * Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
     7→ * modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions
     8→ * are met:
     9→ *
    10→ * 1. Redistributions of source code must retain the above copyright
    11→ *    notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer.
    12→ *
    13→ * 2. Redistributions in binary form must reproduce the above
    14→ *    copyright notice, this list of conditions and the following
    15→ *    disclaimer in the documentation and/or other materials provided
    16→ *    with the distribution.
    17→ *
    18→ * 3. Neither the name of the copyright holder nor the names of its
    19→ *    contributors may be used to endorse or promote products derived
    20→ *    from this software without specific prior written permission.
    21→ *
    22→ * THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS
    23→ * "AS IS" AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT
    24→ * LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS
    25→ * FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE ARE DISCLAIMED. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE
    26→ * COPYRIGHT HOLDER OR CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT,
    27→ * INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES
    28→ * (INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR
    29→ * SERVICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR PROFITS; OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION)
    30→ * HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT,
    31→ * STRICT LIABILITY, OR TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE OR OTHERWISE)
    32→ * ARISING IN ANY WAY OUT OF THE USE OF THIS SOFTWARE, EVEN IF ADVISED
    33→ * OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
    34→ */
    35→
    36→#ifndef _TEST_MATH_H_
    37→#define _TEST_MATH_H_
    38→
    39→#ifndef _DEFAULT_SOURCE
    40→#define _DEFAULT_SOURCE
    41→#endif
    42→#include <math.h>
    43→#include <float.h>
    44→#include <complex.h>
    45→#include <inttypes.h>
    46→#include <stdio.h>
    47→#include <string.h>
    48→#include <assert.h>
    49→
    50→#if ((__GNUC__ == 4 && __GNUC_MINOR__ >= 2) || __GNUC__ > 4)
    51→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wpragmas"
    52→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wunknown-warning-option"
    53→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Woverflow"
    54→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wliteral-range"
    55→#pragma GCC diagnostic ignored "-Wdouble-promotion"
    56→#endif
    57→
    58→#ifdef __RX__
    59→#define SKIP_DENORM
    60→#endif
    61→
    62→#ifdef _RX_PID
    63→#define TEST_CONST
    64→#else
    65→#define TEST_CONST const
    66→#endif
    67→
    68→#define count(a)           (sizeof(a) / sizeof(a[0]))
    69→#define _MATH_CONCAT(a, b) a##b
    70→#define MATH_CONCAT(a, b)  _MATH_CONCAT(a, b)
    71→#define _MATH_STRING(a)    #a
    72→#define MATH_STRING(a)     _MATH_STRING(a)
    73→
    74→#ifdef SKIP_DENORM
    75→#define MY_ABS(x)                         \
    76→    ({                                    \
    77→        __typeof(x) __tmp__ = (x);        \
    78→        __tmp__ < 0 ? -__tmp__ : __tmp__; \
    79→    })
    80→#define SKIP_DENORM32(x)  (MY_ABS(x) < MIN_BINARY32)
    81→#define SKIP_DENORM64(x)  (MY_ABS(x) < MIN_BINARY64)
    82→#define SKIP_DENORM80(x)  (MY_ABS(x) < MIN_BINARY80)
    83→#define SKIP_DENORM128(x) (MY_ABS(x) < MIN_BINARY128)
    84→#else
    85→#define SKIP_DENORM32(x)  0
    86→#define SKIP_DENORM64(x)  0
    87→#define SKIP_DENORM80(x)  0
    88→#define SKIP_DENORM128(x) 0
    89→#endif
    90→
    91→#define SKIP_CDENORM32(z)  (SKIP_DENORM32(creal32(z)) || SKIP_DENORM32(cimag32(z)))
    92→#define SKIP_CDENORM64(z)  (SKIP_DENORM64(creal64(z)) || SKIP_DENORM64(cimag64(z)))
    93→#define SKIP_CDENORM80(z)  (SKIP_DENORM80(creal80(z)) || SKIP_DENORM80(cimag80(z)))
    94→#define SKIP_CDENORM128(z) (SKIP_DENORM128(creal128(z)) || SKIP_DENORM128(cimag128(z)))
    95→
    96→#define MAX_ULP            9999
    97→#define INV_ULP            10000
    98→typedef int32_t ulp_t;
    99→#define PRIdULP PRId32
   100→
   101→typedef const struct {
   102→    const char *name;
   103→    ulp_t       b32;
   104→    ulp_t       b64;
   105→    ulp_t       b80;
   106→    ulp_t       b128;
   107→} math_ulps_t;
   108→
   109→static TEST_CONST math_ulps_t math_ulps[];
   110→
   111→ulp_t                         max_ulp;
   112→
   113→#include "test-ulp.h"
   114→
   115→static inline math_ulps_t *
   116→math_find_ulps(void)
   117→{
   118→    const char *name = MATH_STRING(TEST_FUNC);
   119→    for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(math_ulps) / sizeof(math_ulps[0]); i++)
   120→        if (strcmp(name, math_ulps[i].name) == 0)
   121→            return &math_ulps[i];
   122→    assert(0);
   123→    return &math_ulps[0];
   124→}
   125→
   126→static inline ulp_t
   127→math_find_ulp_binary32(void)
   128→{
   129→    math_ulps_t *ulps = math_find_ulps();
   130→    return ulps->b32;
   131→}
   132→
   133→static inline ulp_t
   134→math_find_ulp_binary64(void)
   135→{
   136→    math_ulps_t *ulps = math_find_ulps();
   137→    return ulps->b64;
   138→}
   139→
   140→static inline ulp_t
   141→math_find_ulp_binary80(void)
   142→{
   143→    math_ulps_t *ulps = math_find_ulps();
   144→    return ulps->b80;
   145→}
   146→
   147→static inline int
   148→math_find_ulp_binary128(void)
   149→{
   150→    math_ulps_t *ulps = math_find_ulps();
